name: styles-bundle
summary: Enqueue a CSS bundle on front-end and/or admin via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that hooks both wp_enqueue_scripts and admin_enqueue_scripts to enqueue a stylesheet.
problem: You have a built CSS bundle that needs to be enqueued in WordPress and you want a single, lifecycle-managed class doing it.
appliesWhen: You ship a built CSS bundle
You want consistent enqueueing across front-end and admin
avoidWhen: The styles are inline (use wp_add_inline_style directly)
The styles ride along with a JS bundle that already imports them
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Hardcoded version string by default — replace with file-mtime or a build-time constant for cache busting.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that hooks wp_enqueue_scripts and admin_enqueue_scripts
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Edit shouldEnqueue() to scope where the stylesheet loads
Replace the hardcoded version with a file-mtime or build constant

srcPath.description: Path to the CSS bundle relative to plugin root
